text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check small pieces of code, like functions or methods, in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of a system work together correctly. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps design better software and catch problems early. By having a solid set of tests, programmers gain confidence in their code. They can make changes or add new features knowing that if something breaks, the tests will alert them immediately. This reduces bugs and improves overall quality.
